title: Roghni Naan

seo.title: How to Make Roghni Naan — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Roghni Naan. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Roghni Naan — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Roghni Naan is a classic Pakistani flatbread, rich and soft, often enjoyed with curries and kebabs. Its signature texture comes from milk and yogurt, giving it a slightly chewy yet tender crumb. Adorned with sesame seeds, this naan offers a delightful aroma and a subtly nutty flavor that complements any meal perfectly.

ingredients.item: Black cumin seeds (kalonji) or sesame seeds

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm milk, sugar, and instant yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. This indicates the yeast is active. If it does not foam, your yeast may be old or the milk not warm enough.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the all-purpose flour, salt, yogurt, and 2 tablespoons of ghee or melted butter to the yeast mixture. Start mixing with a spoon or your hands. Gradually add water, a little at a time, until a soft, slightly sticky dough forms. Avoid adding too much water at once.

instructions.title: Knead and First Rise
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape the Naans
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough to release the air. Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each ball into an oval or teardrop shape, about 1/4 inch thick. Do not roll them too thin.

instructions.title: Bake the Naans
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C) with a baking stone or an inverted baking sheet inside. Brush the surface of each naan with the lightly beaten egg wash and sprinkle generously with black cumin or sesame seeds. Carefully place the naans directly onto the hot baking stone or sheet. Bake for 3-5 minutes, or until puffed up and lightly golden brown on the top. You may need to broil for the last minute for a nice char.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the naans from the oven and immediately brush with a little extra ghee or melted butter for a beautiful shine and added richness. Serve the Roghni Naan hot alongside your favorite Pakistani curries, stews, or kebabs. Enjoy the soft, fragrant bread with your meal.

tips: For a truly authentic taste and texture, ensure your yogurt and milk are at room temperature before mixing them into the dough.
Do not over-knead the dough after the first rise; just gently punch it down and shape to maintain its soft, airy structure.
Serve Roghni Naan immediately after baking for the best experience. If reheating, sprinkle with a little water and warm in a hot oven for a few minutes.
article: Roghni Naan is a beloved Pakistani Baking dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
